preview_text:
The model favors Spain at 48%, with France positioned as a clear underdog at 22%. The remaining probability mass—nearly 30%—clusters in a draw outcome, suggesting the match could settle either way beyond a straightforward Spain victory.

The 26-percentage-point gap between the favorite and underdog indicates a competitive but asymmetric contest. A draw sits between the two outright results in likelihood, which means the match shape is moderately dispersed across all three outcomes rather than heavily concentrated. Spain enters as the likeliest single result, but the combined probability of a France win or draw (52%) remains substantial.

No sportsbook consensus is currently available. The Polymarket prices—assigning 100% to Spain and 0% to both France and a draw—are extreme outliers and do not represent a functional market for analytical comparison. The dashboard makes no claim about which source reflects true match probability; such extreme pricing typically indicates illiquidity or data anomalies rather than informed consensus.
